what_it_does: Defines a set of adapter functions under global.DNA.faceRecord that transform raw API records (sessions, channels, board rows, transcript search) into normalized render records intended for renderArchetype.
observations: All adapters return a nested identity object plus shaped fields.
boardRecord explicitly converts a grouped dictionary into a declared array of group objects.
channelGraph accepts channel.members as either an array or an object and derives edges for members, coordinators, and promotions.
transcriptRecord has two distinct output modes: raw session-node constellation and grounded theme/claim clusters.
The file includes inline slot_map binding hints (e.g. where←cwd, name←title).
No imports or dependencies; it attaches to a global DNA namespace via an IIFE.
rel() is a very crude timestamp formatter, not a true relative-time calculator.
Human-readable labels are preferred over raw ids/addresses throughout.
standouts: The dual-mode transcriptRecord adapter with provenance and a degraded flag.
The explicit dict-to-array normalization in boardRecord.
The channelGraph edge construction that handles two member input shapes.
conventions: Uses an IIFE that writes to global.DNA.faceRecord.
Camel-case function names with defensive defaulting of arguments.
Comments describe render-slot bindings rather than implementation details.
Prefers human labels over raw identifiers in output records.
concerns: rel() only reformats strings and does not compute real relative time.
shortCwd() can produce ambiguous labels when different paths share the same last two segments.
There is no input validation beyond truthiness checks and coercion.
The global DNA namespace could collide with other files.
channelGraph hub label is hardcoded to 'fabric'.
notes: A thin, host-agnostic translation layer that keeps API-record shaping out of the host and close to the render archetype bindings.
questions: What exact fields does renderArchetype expect from each returned record shape?
How is the 'warmth' field consumed by the channel-graph organism?
For channel members passed as an object, are the keys the intended node identifiers or just membership flags?
What is DNA.org.interfaceFragment and where is it defined?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it turns raw route records into shaped records for renderArchetype.
apparent_themes: Separation of host code from render/DNA logic
intention_signals: The file tries to create a stable, reusable boundary between backend API shapes and frontend render archetypes so host code only fetches and mounts.
novelty: The explicit normalization of grouped dictionaries into declared arrays and the transcript adapter's dual-mode output with provenance/degraded metadata.
contribution: A set of shape-normalizing adapters that convert API-specific records into render-ready records with human labels, grouped arrays, graph edges, and provenance metadata.
summary_for_embedding: face-adapters.js is a collection of host-shape adapter functions under global.DNA.faceRecord that convert raw API records for sessions, channels, board rows, and transcript search results into normalized render records consumed by renderArchetype.
intention_for_embedding: To keep field-shaping logic out of host code by centralizing API-to-archetype record transformation in a reusable adapter layer.

symbols.does: Formats a timestamp into a simplified date-time string.
symbols.description: Strips the 'T' separator and trailing seconds/timezone from an ISO-like timestamp, returning a short display string; falls back to 'unknown' for missing input. It is a string-formatting helper rather than a true relative-time calculator.

symbols.does: Abbreviates a filesystem path to its last two non-empty segments.
symbols.description: Splits the cwd by '/', filters empty segments, and returns the last two joined by '/' (or the original value if empty). Used to produce a compact human location label for session cards.

symbols.does: Converts a raw /api/sessions record into a session-card render record.
symbols.description: Builds an identity object containing id, name, title, abbreviated cwd, state, last_activity, a generated summary, a facts array, an openLabel, and a session:// address. The returned shape is intended to bind to session-card slots such as where, name, is, facts, and ground.

symbols.does: Converts raw channel data into a hub-network render record.
symbols.description: Maps channels into scored/warmth nodes, normalizes posts to a 0–1 score, and constructs edges for members, coordinators, and promoted_to relationships. It accepts members as either an array or an object and returns nodes, edges, and a hardcoded fabric hub.

symbols.does: Groups raw board rows into a render-ready declared array.
symbols.description: Normalizes board rows, groups them by type or an opts.groupBy key, and explicitly converts the resulting dictionary into an array of group objects with human labels, counts, and item arrays. Returns an identity, the groups array, and a total count.

symbols.does: Converts a transcript-search API response into a constellation render record.
symbols.description: Branches into grounded mode (theme/claim clusters with provenance metadata) when an asset or themes are present, otherwise returns raw mode session star-nodes weighted by hits and brightness by score. Includes a degraded flag for lexical fallback or unavailable semantic search, and always uses human labels rather than raw ids.
